task.title: Integrate declarative secret management
task.objective: Commit discoverable secret declarations while keeping all values in external providers.
acceptance_criteria: SecretSpec is pinned in the Nix development shell.
A disposable temporary-provider fixture verifies declaration resolution and command injection without printing the value.
The complete local quality gate passes.
The trust boundary and deferred SDK integration are documented.

attempts.summary: Ran the integration test before adding the package or declaration.
attempts.diagnostic: The test exited at the SecretSpec executable check because the tool was absent from the pinned development shell.
attempts.patch: Added the integration test first to establish the missing-tool failure.

attempts.summary: Added SecretSpec, a committed declaration, developer recipes, documentation, and an architecture decision.
attempts.patch: Pinned SecretSpec in flake.nix; added secretspec.toml, tests/secretspec-integration.sh, just recipes, README guidance, and ADR 0001.

outcome.summary: Vela now has a verified declaration-versus-storage boundary without reading or committing real credential values.

lessons: Use an established provider abstraction instead of implementing credential storage inside the assistant kernel.
A temporary provider file plus an unset parent variable can verify secret injection without exposing a real credential.
Add real declarations only alongside the runtime capability that consumes them.
